---
title: "Group Project"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---

> Work with your group across three afternoon sessions to take a real
> dataset from raw CSVs to a short presentation.

## Datasets

Your group will work with one of two datasets, both included in the
participant codespace under `data/`:

- **Group A — Yogurt study** (`data/group_a_yogurt/`): participant
  metadata, qPCR results, and Luminex cytokine measurements from a
  randomized study of yogurt consumption.
- **Group B — Menstruation study** (`data/group_b_menstruation/`):
  longitudinal sampling across the menstrual cycle, with participant
  metadata, Luminex cytokines, and flow cytometry.

Pick a biological question that interests your group and build toward a
short presentation by Friday.

## Session 1 — Wednesday: Get to know your dataset

The first session is exploratory. You're not analyzing yet — you're
building a mental map of the dataset so you can decide what's worth asking.

Read the CSVs into R, look at the tables, and discuss as a group:

- What does each table contain, and what does one row mean?
- How are the tables linked together?
- Which variables look interesting? What looks suspicious or missing?
- What biological question might your group want to explore by Thursday?

Full prompts live in `group_project/README_session1.md` in your codespace.

## Session 2 — Thursday: Table 1 and group comparisons

Pick a question. Pick the groups you want to compare. Then build a Table 1
with **gtsummary** to describe your participants, and use **ggpubr** to
show comparisons with p-values.

By the end of Thursday you should have:

- A Table 1 you would feel OK putting in a paper
- One or two ggpubr figures of the comparisons you care about
- A clear sense of what your group will say on Friday

Full prompts live in `group_project/README_session2.md` in your codespace.

## Session 3 — Friday: Present your findings

Each group gives a short presentation (about five minutes) to the rest of
the workshop. The goal isn't to impress — it's to share what you found,
what surprised you, and what you'd want to do next.

Bring:

- Your question
- One or two takeaways from your Table 1
- The figure that best tells your story
- A short list of limitations and next steps

Full prompts live in `group_project/README_session3.md` in your codespace.
